formula_note: Low = 16,000 AK + 2,297 Polish 1st Army bridgehead deaths + 2,000 German/auxiliary deaths. Best substitutes 3,000 German deaths. High substitutes 10,000 German KIA as a legacy upper bound but excludes the disputed additional 7,000 German missing.
uncertainty_note: Largest uncertainty is German KIA. Aggregate military deaths mix Polish and German victim sides, so side fields are left null at estimate level and preserved in the underlying claims.
quality_note: Polish-side deaths are fairly stable; German-side deaths remain contested.

victim_side: Warsaw civilians (mostly Poles, plus hidden Jews not demonstrably in armed service)
formula_note: Best estimate follows the dominant 150,000 scholarly cluster. High estimate follows the 180,000–200,000 upper cluster, with 200,000 retained as a conservative high bound.
uncertainty_note: Direct civilian deaths are much better constrained than combatant exposure, but still rounded historical estimates rather than complete named-victim counts.
quality_note: Civilian loss estimates are the best-triangulated part of the episode.

formula_note: No open-access source found a reliable total direct-death series for persons whose status remained unresolved between civilian and combatant.
uncertainty_note: Thousands of Jews were hiding in Warsaw; some joined armed units, some remained civilians, and a clean unknown-status death total was not identified. Core DR therefore uses strict exclusion rather than silent allocation.
quality_note: Classification ambiguity is real, but quantification is weak.

formula_note: 63-day episode converted to 2.0698 months. Low scenario uses 57,000 average combatants (32,000 insurgents + 25,000 Germans). Best uses 80,000 average combatants. High uses 100,000 average combatants, anchored by the ENRS c.50,000-per-side graphic. Polish 1st Army bridgehead participation is treated as absorbed within the best/high average-combatant scenarios rather than separately added.
uncertainty_note: No direct daily order-of-battle series was found. This is a source-anchored scenario reconstruction rather than a directly observed person-month count.
quality_note: Adequate for bounded DR sensitivity, but not a direct archival total.

formula_note: 63-day episode converted to 2.0698 months. Start stock = 720,000 civilians in left-bank Warsaw. End-stage stock approximated at 155,519 to 200,000 civilians still present in the city at the start of final expulsion. Best scenario assumes average exposed civilian population of 460,000 across the episode; low/high scenarios use 350,000 and 550,000 averages to reflect faster or slower depopulation.
uncertainty_note: Civilian person-months are highly sensitive to the timing of expulsions and intra-city refugee movements. These are bounded scenarios, not observed daily headcounts.
quality_note: Best available denominator approach, but necessarily modeled.

formula_note: DR_v1 = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low scenario pairs mil-deaths low with mil-PM high and civ-deaths high with civ-PM low; best uses all best values; high pairs mil-deaths high with mil-PM low and civ-deaths low with civ-PM high.
uncertainty_note: This is a bounded scenario range, not a statistical confidence interval. The best estimate lies below 1, but the interval crosses 1 because German military deaths and civilian depopulation timing remain uncertain.
quality_note: Interpret with caution: numerators are better constrained than denominators.
